text: the 'time in the market' part is the one thing everyone misses until it is way too late
wait actually—is it really just exponential? feels like it takes forever to even notice it working
that is because the first half is just you staring at a flat line
the curve is brutal at the start. you feel like you are doing nothing for years, and then one day the interest is making more than your paycheck.
that 'crossover' moment is the only reason i keep at it honestly
the real trap is people thinking they can beat the curve by picking stocks. slow and boring wins every single time.
tell that to my portfolio from 2021 lol
does this account for inflation though? usually the math looks great until you adjust for what bread is going to cost in 30 years
it does not. but money under the mattress is losing to inflation every single day anyway, so it is still the better bet.
